summary: CC-0096 flips Trust Flush from opt-in to default-on by adding a defaulting-webhook block that materializes spec.trustFlush={schedule:'0 * * * *', suspend:false} when the field is nil (operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:104-109). The reconciler's nil-branch is reframed as a legacy-bypass path that logs a structured info message, deletes any stray CronJob, and reports TrustFlushReady=True/TrustFlushNotRequired (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush.go:39-56). Field godoc on KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush and TrustFlushSpec is rewritten and the regenerated CRD YAML is in sync. Tests: webhook tests TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec, TestDefault_TrustFlushSet_PreservesExplicitValues, TestValidate_TrustFlushDefaulted_AcceptsHourlySchedule; controller tests TestReconcileTrustFlush_BypassNil_LogsWarning, TestReconcileTrustFlush_WebhookDefaulted_CreatesCronJobAndReportsReady, TestReconcileTrustFlush_SuspendTrue_PreservesCronJobAndReason; integration tests TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedFieldMaterializedAndCronJobCreated, TestIntegration_TrustFlush_PatchToNullReDefaultsAndPreservesCronJob, TestIntegration_TrustFlush_SuspendTruePreservesCronJob. E2E: trust-flush/01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml now patches suspend:true and chainsaw-test.yaml asserts CronJob preservation + no owned Jobs created while suspended; new sibling suite trust-flush-default/ exercises the omitted-field happy path. Docs: keystone-crd.md, keystone-reconciler.md, keystone-e2e-tests.md, quick-start.md, observability.md, advanced-configuration.md, and a new CC-0096 section in keystone-upgrade-flow.md cover the new posture, the suspend opt-out, the no-nil-back contract, and the brownfield-upgrade callout. Unit + controller tests pass locally; envtest integration and chainsaw runs require KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS/kind which the sandbox lacks.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests in api/v1alpha1 pass (go test ./api/v1alpha1/ -count=1 -> ok 0.073s)
Unit tests in internal/controller pass (go test ./internal/controller/ -count=1 -> ok 1.463s)
TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec asserts schedule, suspend, and empty Args after Default() (keystone_webhook_test.go:151-164)
TestDefault_TrustFlushSet_PreservesExplicitValues asserts user-provided schedule, suspend, args survive Default() (keystone_webhook_test.go:168-186)
TestValidate_TrustFlushDefaulted_AcceptsHourlySchedule confirms the webhook->validate handoff accepts the hourly default (keystone_webhook_test.go:1599-1612)
TestReconcileTrustFlush_BypassNil_LogsWarning verifies the bypass log carries the legacy bypass sentinel + namespace + name (reconcile_trustflush_test.go:585-610)
TestReconcileTrustFlush_SuspendTrue_PreservesCronJobAndReason proves CronJob is created with Suspend=true and reason stays TrustFlushReady (reconcile_trustflush_test.go:646-669)
TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedFieldMaterializedAndCronJobCreated asserts persisted spec.trustFlush + CronJob + reason via the real defaulting webhook (integration_test.go:1768-1810)
TestIntegration_TrustFlush_PatchToNullReDefaultsAndPreservesCronJob proves nil-back round-trips and CronJob UID is preserved (integration_test.go:1819-1882)
TestIntegration_TrustFlush_SuspendTruePreservesCronJob replaces the deletion test with the suspend semantics (integration_test.go:1702-1760)
Legacy bypass-path tests are renamed/annotated to reflect their new envtest-only role (reconcile_trustflush_test.go:171-192, :291-296)
Existing trust-flush chainsaw suite re-asserts suspend posture and verifies no CronJob-owned Jobs are created while suspended (tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/chainsaw-test.yaml:139-193)
New trust-flush-default chainsaw suite asserts persisted webhook-defaulted spec, CronJob, reason, and a manual flush job (tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/chainsaw-test.yaml)
envtest integration suite executed end-to-end on a real envtest binary

code_quality_checklist.item: Webhook defaulting block matches the existing Replicas/Bootstrap.Region/AdminUser/Cache.Backend style (keystone_webhook.go:81-109)
Reconciler nil-branch keeps deleteCronJob() helper intact and unchanged for finalizer/legacy paths (reconcile_trustflush.go:148-156)
Status condition reasons are consistent: TrustFlushReady on the production path, TrustFlushNotRequired only on legacy bypass (reconcile_trustflush.go:48-69)
Godoc on KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush + TrustFlushSpec describes default-on posture, suspend opt-out, and no nil-back path (keystone_types.go:75-86, :342-362)
Generated CRD YAML in operators/keystone/config/crd/bases and operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds is regenerated and matches
Every changed file references CC-0096 in comments/tests/docs per project rule 11
No new TrustFlushSuspended reason introduced — suspend keeps reason TrustFlushReady, mirroring the autoscaling-suspended-HPA precedent

security_checklist.item: No new external inputs introduced — defaulting webhook only writes server-controlled values
No secret material exposed in log keys (only namespace/name)
RBAC unchanged — sub-reconciler keeps its existing CronJob verbs (no new privileges)
Cron expression continues to be parsed via cron.ParseStandard with explicit empty-string guard (keystone_webhook.go:251-263)

architecture_checklist.item: Pointer shape preserved — spec.trustFlush remains *TrustFlushSpec, no breaking API shape change
+kubebuilder:default markers on Schedule and Suspend retained as defense-in-depth (keystone_types.go:352, :356)
Single source of truth for the production default lives in the webhook; CRD markers serve only the webhook-bypass path
deleteCronJob is retained for envtest/finalizer paths and not made unreachable
No scope creep: the diff is confined to keystone webhook/types/reconciler + their tests + the relevant docs and e2e suites

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated default values across the webhook block — the schedule/suspend literal is co-located with the materialization (keystone_webhook.go:104-109)
Existing helpers (commonLabels, restrictedSecurityContext, buildDBConnectionEnvVar, EnsureCronJob) are reused unchanged
No premature abstraction — schedule constant is not extracted because it appears in only one Go call site (the webhook block); the validation error message uses a string literal already

fail_fast_checklist.item: validate() rejects empty/invalid cron strings before the reconciler ever sees the object (keystone_webhook.go:251-263)
Reconciler fails fast on EnsureCronJob errors with wrapped 'ensuring trust flush CronJob' context (reconcile_trustflush.go:60-62)
Bypass-path delete errors are wrapped with 'deleting trust flush CronJob' context (reconcile_trustflush.go:45-47)

defensive_checklist.item: Webhook tolerates a nil Spec.TrustFlush and never overwrites a user-set value (keystone_webhook.go:104-109; TestDefault_TrustFlushSet_PreservesExplicitValues)
Reconciler gracefully handles spec.TrustFlush == nil (legacy bypass) instead of nil-derefing
Integration test forces a literal `null` patch via client.RawPatch with MergePatchType to exercise the JSON-null path (integration_test.go:1849-1855)
Chainsaw suspend-step asserts no Jobs are created with the trust-flush CronJob as owner while suspend is true (chainsaw-test.yaml:185-193)

issues_found.description: Bypass-path log structured-keys are missing the explicit reason key called out in the review criteria. The criteria say the bypass log MUST include `"reason":"webhook-bypass"` so it is greppable by log pipelines; the implementation only emits `namespace` and `name` keys, with the bypass sentinel embedded in the human-readable message. A `reason` key is the established convention for differentiating bypass vs production paths in greppable form.
issues_found.fix: Append a `reason` structured key to the Info call, e.g. `log.FromContext(ctx).Info("trust flush legacy bypass: ...", "namespace", keystone.Namespace, "name", keystone.Name, "reason", "webhook-bypass")` and update TestReconcileTrustFlush_BypassNil_LogsWarning to assert the substring `"reason":"webhook-bypass"` (or equivalent funcr-rendered form).
issues_found.evidence:
log.FromContext(ctx).Info(
    "trust flush legacy bypass: spec.trustFlush is nil — webhook defaulting did not run; deleting any existing CronJob (CC-0096, REQ-002)",
    "namespace", keystone.Namespace,
    "name", keystone.Name,
)

issues_found.description: The fixture filename `01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml` is now a misnomer. The file no longer disables (nil) trust flush — it patches `spec.trustFlush.suspend: true`. The header comment, doc table (docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md:845), and chainsaw step comments correctly describe the new behavior, but the filename still reads as a 'disable' patch. This is a minor naming inconsistency that future readers will find confusing when they grep for 'suspend' patches.
issues_found.fix: Rename the file to `01-patch-suspend-trust-flush.yaml` and update the corresponding `patch.file:` reference in tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/chainsaw-test.yaml:144 and the row in docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md:845.
issues_found.evidence:
tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml header now reads:
# Patch to pause trust flush via spec.trustFlush.suspend: true (CC-0057, CC-0096, REQ-006).
...
spec:
  trustFlush:
    suspend: true

issues_found.description: The hourly schedule literal `"0 * * * *"` appears in three places: the +kubebuilder:default marker on TrustFlushSpec.Schedule (keystone_types.go:352), the webhook materialization block (keystone_webhook.go:106), and the `validate()` Required-error message (keystone_webhook.go:255). This mirrors the CC-0084 pattern — DefaultTerminationGracePeriodSeconds and DefaultPreStopSleepSeconds are exported package-level constants explicitly described as 'single source of truth used by both the validating webhook ... and the reconciler' (keystone_webhook.go:28-38). Extracting `DefaultTrustFlushSchedule = "0 * * * *"` would align with that pattern and prevent drift if the cadence is ever revisited.
issues_found.fix: Add `DefaultTrustFlushSchedule = "0 * * * *"` to the existing const block (next to DefaultTerminationGracePeriodSeconds), reference it in Default() and the Required error message, and update test assertions to use the constant.

issues_found.description: The `deleteCronJob` doc comment is still tagged `(CC-0057, REQ-002)` only. After CC-0096 reframed the nil-branch as legacy bypass, this helper is invoked solely from the bypass path and is preserved 'for envtest/legacy callers'. The comment should cite CC-0096 alongside CC-0057 so future readers see the reframed contract without having to git-blame.
issues_found.fix: Update the doc comment to read e.g. `// deleteCronJob ... (CC-0057, CC-0096, REQ-002 — invoked only on the legacy bypass path post-CC-0096).`
issues_found.evidence:
// deleteCronJob deletes the CronJob identified by namespace and name. It is a
// no-op if the CronJob does not exist (CC-0057, REQ-002).

suggested_improvements: Consider asserting on the materialized spec.trustFlush.schedule + spec.trustFlush.suspend in TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue once a webhook-enabled envtest fixture is available there — today the test only loops over condition types and uses a fake client (keystone_controller_test.go:354).

If staggered rollout becomes a real concern, follow up with a Chainsaw upgrade-fixture suite that exercises the brownfield path (operator upgrade -> kubectl patch '{}' -> CronJob created) end-to-end. The current upgrade-flow doc covers the steps but no automated test verifies the timing.

The bypass log uses Info level. logr has no Warning level, but if structured log pipelines key off severity, switching to `Error(nil, ...)` would be the closest equivalent to a warning. Today's Info is acceptable — flagged as a potential follow-up only.
next_steps: Run the integration suite on a host with KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS pointing at envtest binaries to confirm the three new TestIntegration_TrustFlush_* cases pass against a real defaulting webhook.
Run the Chainsaw E2E suite against a kind cluster to confirm both trust-flush/ and trust-flush-default/ pass and do not collide (distinct CR names, distinct keystone DB names already verified).
Optionally apply the four MINOR fixes (structured `reason` log key, fixture rename, DefaultTrustFlushSchedule constant, deleteCronJob comment) before merge.
